subject: Computer Networking
title: Email Addresses Decoded
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Email Addresses Decoded.

hook.text: user@example.com looks simple. But every part has a precise technical meaning.

buildup.text: The local part (before @) identifies the mailbox. The domain part identifies the mail server.

discovery.text: The domain maps to an MX record. The local part is matched by the receiving server to a mailbox.

twist.text: Plus addressing lets you add tags: user+shopping@example.com still reaches user. Great for filtering.

climax.text: Internationalized addresses now support non-Latin scripts. Email in Arabic or Chinese is valid.

punchline.text: The @ splits who from where. That's all email needs.

quiz.question: What does the domain part of an email address identify?
options: The mail server that handles delivery
The folder to store the email in
